title: Window Seat Storage Bench from One Sheet of Plywood
summary: A long, low storage bench built from a single sheet of plywood ripped into strips, with base and cove moulding for a built-in look — genuinely economical for its finished size.
description:
This bench is sized to stretch nearly wall to wall while using just one sheet of plywood, ripped into three 15-3/4"-wide strips that supply every structural part — top, dividers, and shelves. Drill pocket holes along the top edge of the dividers first and attach them to the underside of the top, using the 1x2 trim or shelf pieces cut to length as temporary spacers to keep divider placement even without measuring each one individually.

The 1x2 trim glued and nailed to the underside of the top comes next, followed by the shelf boards — each one also pocket-holed and spaced with scrap cutoffs rather than a tape measure for every single piece, which is faster and keeps spacing consistent bay to bay.

**The back panel does double duty structurally**, attached with glue to every fixed shelf and divider rather than just the outer frame — this is what keeps a bench this long from racking under a sitter's weight at one end. Base moulding and cove trim finish the piece last, giving a plain plywood box the look of true built-in millwork despite being a genuinely freestanding, single-sheet build.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)
1/4" plywood or hardboard, panel for back
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)
3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)
3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)
1/4" plywood or hardboard, panel for back

steps.title: Attach the dividers to the top
steps.body: Drill three 3/4" pocket holes along the top edge of each divider. Attach to the underside of the top with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, using cut-to-length spacers to guide placement.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)

steps.title: Attach the top trim
steps.body: Glue and nail the 1x2 trim to the underside of the top with 1-1/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on both ends of each shelf board, three per end. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, using cut-to-length spacers to guide placement.
materials: 3/4" plywood, ripped to 15-3/4" strips, full sheet (AC or better, hardwood, or MDF)

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back panel with glue and 3/4" nails or staples, fastening to all fixed shelves and dividers.
materials: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, panel for back

steps.title: Add the moulding
steps.body: Attach the base moulding and top cove moulding to the front with brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Long window seat storage bench built from one sheet of plywood
